notes: R3 (post-connect prompt) is the discriminating eval: baseline scored 1/3 because the original workflow text told the creator to immediately loop on channels_recv, overriding any user-decides-the-role behavior. The new explicit rule fixes this.

R1 (send-then-recv) and R2 (recv-after-act) both pass in both configs. The skill's original workflow steps ("channels_recv (loop) to wait") already implicitly encoded these patterns — the new explicit rules reinforce them but do not change behavior in this scenario.
Recommendation: keep R1/R2 rules as they make the implicit explicit and protect against future workflow rewrites; iterate on R3 wording if any production miss occurs.
Single-run evals so stddev is noisy. The 78% ± 38% on baseline reflects pass/fail on the single discriminating case across 3 evals, not within-eval variance.
